 {"id":"bd-0nes","title":"Enable parallel test execution with proper isolation","description":"Run tests in parallel for faster execution.

Components:
- run_parallel_tests(): Execute tests in parallel with isolation
- Per-test temp directories (already namespaced)
- Proper cleanup on interrupt
- Aggregate results from parallel runs
- --jobs N option for parallelism control

Acceptance:
- Tests run in parallel without interference
- Total test time reduced by 50%+
- Results correctly aggregated","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:03.626856246-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:41:31.687527502-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:41:31.687527502-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented parallel test execution with job limiting.  {"id":"bd-0vm5","title":"Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (Worktrees, GraphQL, Work Items)","description":"# Phase 1: Core Infrastructure

## Overview
Foundation layer providing the command skeleton, efficient API access, work item discovery, and safe workspace isolation. Everything else builds on this.

## Why This Phase First
- cmd_review is the entry point for all review functionality
- GraphQL batching reduces API calls from O(n) to O(n/25) - critical for 50+ repos
- Work item model enables intelligent prioritization
- Worktree isolation is the safety foundation

## Components

### 1.1 cmd_review Command Skeleton
- New command handler in ru script
- Argument parsing (--plan/--apply, --parallel, --dry-run, etc.)
- Review lock mechanism (prevent concurrent runs)
- Phase orchestration (discovery → preparation → orchestration → apply)

### 1.2 GraphQL Batched Discovery
- Query up to 25 repos in single API call using aliases
- Extract issues + PRs with full metadata (labels, dates, draft status)
- Filter archived/forked repos
- Chunk repos into batches for large repo lists

### 1.3 Work Item Model & Priority Scoring
- Individual issue/PR scoring (not repo-level)
- Score components:
  * Type importance (PR=20, Issue=10, Draft PR penalty=-15)
  * Label priority (security/critical=50, bug/urgent=30, enhancement=10)
  * Age factor (older bugs more urgent, very old features penalized)
  * Recency bonus (recent activity = engagement)
  * Staleness penalty (already reviewed = -20)
- Priority levels: CRITICAL (150+), HIGH (100-149), NORMAL (50-99), LOW (0-49)

### 1.4 Worktree Preparation
- Create isolated worktree per repo under $RU_STATE_DIR/worktrees/$RUN_ID/
- Branch naming: ru/review/$RUN_ID/$repo_id
- Respect branch pins from repo spec
- Create .ru/ directory for artifacts
- Refuse to run on dirty main worktrees

### 1.5 State Persistence with Atomic Writes
- Global lock file with flock for concurrent access safety
- Atomic JSON writes (write to temp, mv to final)
- review-state.json: repo outcomes, item outcomes, run history
- review-questions.json: pending question queue

### 1.6 Repo Digest Cache
- Store cached codebase understanding in ~/.local/state/ru/repo-digests/
- Metadata includes last commit SHA, last update timestamp
- On review: copy cached digest to worktree/.ru/repo-digest.md
- After review: update cache with new digest

## Exit Criteria
- `ru review --dry-run` shows discovered work items with priority scores
- Worktrees created correctly for top-priority repos
- GraphQL batching verified (< 5 API calls for 100 repos)
- State files written atomically with proper locking

## Estimated Effort
~500-700 lines of Bash","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:15:54.111291783-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:00:31.126307352-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:00:31.126307352-05:00","close_reason":"All Phase 1 components complete: cmd_review skeleton (bd-mnu9), GraphQL discovery (bd-ff8h), priority scoring (bd-5jph), worktree preparation (bd-zlws)"}
 {"id":"bd-1","title":"Phase 1: Project Setup","description":"**EPIC: Project Foundation & Repository Structure**

## Goal
Establish the foundational file structure, versioning, and CI/CD scaffolding that all subsequent work depends on.

## Rationale
A well-organized project structure from day one prevents technical debt accumulation. The giil project demonstrated that a clean, minimal structure with clear separation of concerns makes maintenance and contribution easier. This phase creates the skeleton that gives the project its identity.

## Key Decisions Encoded
- VERSION file as single source of truth (not package.json, not embedded in script)
- MIT License for maximum adoption
- Examples directory for user guidance WITHOUT polluting user's XDG config
- No je_*.txt files in repo (those were development artifacts from original sync script)

## Success Criteria
- Repository has all foundational files in place
- CI can at least do basic syntax validation
- A contributor can clone and understand the structure immediately","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:15:08.191734693-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 setup completed: VERSION, LICENSE, .gitignore exist. Remaining items (bd-104, bd-105, bd-106) can be done in parallel.","labels":["foundation","setup"]}
